author: codex

scope.question: Against current post-wave28 validation state, after excluding applied relation batches017-036 and missing-target repairs, decide the next best data cleanup bucket versus scoped validator rule patch for remaining unexpected_relation_endpoint_shape rows.
scope.write_scope_observed: Created exactly this prep artifact under data/review_notes/.

post_wave28_context.summary: Post-wave28 generated-output checkpoint is complete. LOG reports validate still exits 1 for known backlog issues and remains at 1,609 issues, including duplicate_global_identity=1569 and unexpected_relation_endpoint_shape=40. Wave29 dry-run reports nothing to ingest, so provider ingest lane is idle/pool-exhausted.
post_wave28_context.note: This task did not rerun materialize, export-jsonl, validate, or provider-backed commands.

excluded_repairs: retired override reasons mentioning missing-target or target repair
exclusion_model.implementation: Read canonical records through readCanonicalRecords(), removed known batch017-036 and missing-target repair submission/record ids from retired overrides, batch prep files, and batch submission journals, then ran relationEndpointShapeIssue() in-process against the remaining relation records. This did not invoke the validate CLI.

remaining_bucket_counts_after_exclusions.tie_break_note: Three buckets tie at count 2. The deterministic sorted inventory places has_timeline_event metric_claim->event first, and this shape has direct prior cleanup precedent in relation cleanup batches026 and 032.

recommendation.reason: The two remaining rows use a metric_claim as the subject of has_timeline_event. Metric claims are values or measurements, not durable timeline owners. The cited source links a procurement amount metric to committee and board approval dates, so the relationship should be represented as event/context supporting the metric claim rather than widening has_timeline_event to accept metric_claim subjects.
recommendation.why_not_validator_rule_change: A validator rule patch admitting has_timeline_event metric_claim->event would remove the same two issues, but it would validate metric-owned chronology. Prior next7 triage and applied batches026/032 explicitly kept metric_claim out of has_timeline_event subjects and used supports event->metric_claim replacements to preserve source-backed timeline context. Keeping that boundary preserves the validator signal.

proposed_mechanism.replace_with: Two relation submissions with relation_kind supports, reversed to event -> metric_claim, preserving the same source-backed approval-date evidence and the metric claim context.

representative_records.display_name: Procurement action scheduled for Board meeting 9/21/22
representative_records.validator_message: Relation kind has_timeline_event expects project|route|corridor|entity|source|event -> event, got metric_claim -> event.

payload_summary.description: Proposed estimated expenditure for 1 action under Schedule H: Modifications to Personal/Miscellaneous Service Contracts.

The Procurement Agenda this month includes 1 action for a proposed estimated expenditure of $8.2M.
Schedule H: Modifications to Personal/Miscellaneous Service Contracts 1 $ 8.2 M

recommended_replacement.description: The September 21, 2022 Board meeting approval context supports the Schedule H $8.2M procurement expenditure metric claim.

representative_records.validator_message: Relation kind has_timeline_event expects project|route|corridor|entity|source|event -> event, got metric_claim -> event.

precedent_evidence.summary: Earlier triage and applied cleanup batches treated has_timeline_event metric_claim->event as data cleanup, not a validator rule patch. Batches026 and 032 retired malformed metric-claim-owned timeline relations and replaced them with supports event->metric_claim rows.
precedent_evidence.why_same_mechanism_applies: The meeting_doc_95301 rows likewise link an event date to a metric claim rather than making the metric claim a timeline-bearing subject.

command_evidence.command: rg --files data/review_notes data/submissions data/submission-overrides | sort | rg 'relation.*(035|036)|batch03[56]|missing.*target|target_repair|repair'

command_evidence.output_excerpt: retired.json contains batch035 retire reasons for sub_e688683abfd90042 and sub_e38ca2e1f6405d00, and batch036 retire reasons for sub_1e3da7df51269b6f and sub_3bd1215592595d96. Batch035/036 replacement journals are present. LOG records the wave28 checkpoint and unexpected_relation_endpoint_shape=40.

Very dirty worktree with existing modified retired.json and relation validator files plus many untracked review notes/submissions; data/review_notes/codex-relation-rule-or-data-next13-prep.json did not exist before this task.

command_evidence.command: bun --eval '<readCanonicalRecords + relationEndpointShapeIssue inventory excluding batches017-036 and missing-target repairs>'
command_evidence.output_excerpt: excluded_submission_count=179; excluded_record_count=91; total=40; top buckets: has_timeline_event metric_claim->event=2, implemented_by route->entity=2, part_of_program event->entity=2

p003_c0001 contains 'Board Action Order To Date Approval Info Other 1 Committee 9/19/22 2 Board 9/21/22'; p003_c0005 says the purpose is to obtain Board approval and inform the MTAHQ Committee of procurement actions.

command_evidence.command: bun --eval '<print canonical records for relation_meeting-doc-95301-procurement-board-meeting, relation_meeting-doc-95301-procurement-committee-meeting, metric/event/source endpoints>'
command_evidence.output_excerpt: Confirmed both relations have relation_kind has_timeline_event with subject metric_meeting-doc-95301-8-2m-procurement and event objects. Metric payload is procurement_expenditure, raw_value_text $8.2M. Event endpoints are Board Meeting - September 21, 2022 and MTAHQ Committee Meeting - September 19, 2022.

has_metric event->metric_claim: ok
related_to metric_claim->event: ok
related_to event->metric_claim: ok
includes source->metric_claim: ok
has_claim event->claim: ok

command_evidence.command: rg -n 'meeting-doc-95301|95301|metric_claim->event|metric_claim -> event|has_timeline_event metric' data/review_notes data/submissions data/submission-overrides/retired.json
command_evidence.output_excerpt: Prior next7 triage says do not widen has_timeline_event to accept metric_claim subjects; batch026 and batch032 retired metric_claim->event timeline rows and replaced them with supports event->metric_claim.

command_evidence.output: No whitespace warnings printed; command exited 1 because --no-index reports differences for the added file.

bun --eval '<print canonical records for relation_meeting-doc-95301-procurement-board-meeting, relation_meeting-doc-95301-procurement-committee-meeting, metric/event/source endpoints>'
rg -n 'meeting-doc-95301|95301|metric_claim->event|metric_claim -> event|has_timeline_event metric' data/review_notes data/submissions data/submission-overrides/retired.json
bun --eval '<concise bucket-count inventory excluding batches017-036 and missing-target repairs>'
next_action: Prepare a narrow data cleanup batch retiring the two meeting_doc_95301 has_timeline_event metric_claim->event submissions and replacing them with supports event->metric_claim submissions. Do not mutate validator rules for this bucket.
